title: Analysis: The Islamic State’s targeting of a Chinese mine in Congo proves the group is a resilient threat
description:
The Islamic State’s Central Africa Province (ISCAP), known locally as the Allied Democratic Forces (ADF), raided and burned down a Chinese-owned mine in eastern Congo earlier this month. The raid, which was far from the jihadist group’s normal area of operation, highlights both ISCAP’s threats to Congo’s mining sector and its resilience after nearly five years of a sustained military operation against it.

title: Spanish woman, 25, dies by legal euthanasia in case that drew national spotlight

title: Iran and the US harden their positions over talks to end the nearly month-old war

title: Rubio tries to sell Iran war to skeptical G7 after Trump insults allies
description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is in France meeting his Group of Seven foreign minister counterparts Friday after President Donald Trump attacked NATO countries over a reluctance or refusal to take part in the Iran war, a conflict that some of America’s closest allies have met with deep skepticism. Trump’s vitriolic comments about NATO during a Cabinet meeting Thursday will likely make it an even tougher task. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Ed

title: Bab al-Mandeb Strait: Another key shipping route under threat
description: Known as the "Gate of Tears" in Arabic, the Strait of Bab al-Mandeb is a narrow waterway at the southern tip of the Red Sea, connecting it with the Gulf of Aden in the Indian Ocean… A crucial passage that Iran has threatened to target if US forces launch a ground assault on its territory.

title: Fifteen years after fall of Gaddafi, Libyan city of Benghazi reborn
description: Fifteen years after the revolution that deposed Libyan dictator Muammar Gaddafi, our team reports from the eastern city of Benghazi. Although the security situation there is now relatively stable, reunification does not seem to be on the immediate agenda, with Libya still ruled by two different governments.

title: Europeans to press US Rubio over war in the Middle East at G7 meeting
description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Friday arrived at a meeting of G7 foreign ministers in France for talks expected to be dominated by tensions over the war against Iran. Making his first trip abroad since the US-Israeli war on Iran broke out on February 28, Rubio is expected to be pressed by European and other allies over American strategy for the further conduct of the conflict which has led to a spike in global energy prices. title: How Israel's Mossad recruits spies in Iran
description: Israel's secret services have launched a campaign calling on the Iranian people to contact them and provide any information they may have about the Revolutionary Guards and the regime’s leaders. However, those who speak out risk punishment – even death. Our colleagues at France 2 take a look at what tactics Israel is employing and its overall motives.

Also, Ethiopia steps up its bid to join the World Trade Organization, over two decades on since applying for accession. Plus we take a look at the role of accessible and inclusive cybersecurity.

title: Strikes continue hitting Iran-backed Iraqi militias, Kataib Hezbollah declares temporary US embassy ceasefire

Unclaimed but likely American airstrikes have continued against Iran-backed Iraqi militias, with one strike reportedly killing a senior Kataib Hezbollah commander. The US and Kataib Hezbollah have subsequently agreed to a temporary, conditional pause in attacks on the US Embassy in Baghdad, though other hostilities in the country continue.
The post Strikes continue hitting Iran-backed Iraqi militias, Kataib Hezbollah declares temporary US embassy

title: Iranian drones and missiles hit ports, oil and gas facilities, and US bases in the region (March 17-19 updates)
description:
Between March 17 and March 19, Iran and its allied militias continued launching drones, missiles, and rockets across the Middle East. The attacks targeted energy infrastructure, airports, military bases, diplomatic facilities, and residential areas in Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, and Iraq.
The post Iranian drones and missiles hit ports, oil and gas facilities, and US bases in the region (March 17-19 updates) app

title: US judge temporarily halts Trump-era sanctions on AI firm Anthropic
description: A US federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked sanctions imposed by the Trump administration on AI company Anthropic, saying the measures likely violated the law. The ruling freezes a Pentagon designation of the firm as a national security risk, protecting it while the government considers an emergency appeal.

title: Middle East live: Iran launches strikes on Israel and Gulf sites
description: Iran's Revolutionary Guards said on Friday they conducted missile and drone strikes the previous day against targets in Israel and US-linked military facilities across the Gulf, hitting sites in the UAE, Qatar, Kuwait and Bahrain, including a Patriot air-defence maintenance facility. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

title: 'Return hubs' for migrants clear EU parliament hurdle
description: European lawmakers cleared the way Thursday for tougher penalties for irregular migrants and their potential deportation to so-called "return hubs" outside the bloc. The measures, criticised by human rights groups, are part of a tightening of Europe's immigration rules in response to pressure across the 27-nation bloc to curb migration. FRANCE 24's Dave Keating reports.

description:
According to Yesh Din report, first 25 days of war saw 257 incidents of extremist settler violence and land seizures
The post NGO counts over 10 settler attacks a day amid Iran war; 14 said hurt in latest violence appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Unable to shut down Kan, coalition maneuvers to control public broadcaster’s budget
description:
Bill removing legal safeguards meant to shield outlet from political pressure moved to Knesset committee headed by Netanyahu loyalist, in crusade to dismantle outlet
The post Unable to shut down Kan, coalition maneuvers to control public broadcasters budget appeared first on The Times of Israel.

title: Lebanon declares Iranian ambassador-designate ‘persona non grata’ before Iranian missile explodes over Lebanese airspace
description: An Iranian ballistic missile exploded in Lebanese airspace shortly after Lebanese Foreign Minister Youssef Raggi declared Tehran’s ambassador-designate in the country “persona non grata.” Official silence, coupled with Lebanese reporting that the missile was targeting an American asset in Lebanon, led to speculation that Iran considered Raggi’s move significant and was retaliating for it. However, subsequent reporting and a Lebanese Armed Forces

title: Iran-linked group claims arson attacks in Antwerp and London
description:
On March 23, Ashab al Yamin, a group with ties to the Islamic Republic, took responsibility for attacks in Antwerp and in London. The incidents raise the total number of claimed attacks by the Iran-linked group against Jewish and Western sites to seven. 
The post Iran-linked group claims arson attacks in Antwerp and London appeared first on FDDs Long War Journal.

title: USS Boxer and 11th Marine Expeditionary Unit deploy to Middle East
description: The Pentagon is deploying thousands of additional Marines and sailors to the Middle East.

title: Iran war is not delaying US weapons shipments to Taiwan, officials say
description: The Iran war has not delayed shipments of weapons to Taiwan, Trump administration officials said Tuesday, despite the demands of the intense air campaign.

title: 'Tehran assumes war is behind them:  Iran will not capitulate, Trump has it all wrong'
description: Nadia Massih is pleased to welcome Danny Citrinowicz, Senior Researcher in the Iran and Shi'ite Axis Program at the Institute for National Security Studies. According to Citrinowicz, the trajectory of the war in Iran hinges not on battlefield dominance alone, but on competing perceptions of resilience, legitimacy, and leverage. Despite America's overwhelming military asymmetry, the Iranian regime interprets its endurance as a form of strategic su

title: Iran's chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz: Fertiliser supply chains under strain
description: As tensions between the US, Israel and Iran choke the Strait of Hormuz, cutting global energy and fertiliser flows, markets face rising prices and food insecurity. We discuss the global fallout with Carlos Razo from the UN Conference on Trade and Development (UNCTAD), exploring the risks for the world's poorest economies and the threat of widespread inflation.

title: Russia says report it is sending drones to Iran 'lies'
description: The Kremlin said Thursday that a report in the Financial Times that it was close to completing a shipment of drones to Iran was "lies". Russia is a close ally of Iran, but denies having sent military aid to its partner since the United States and Israel began attacking the country in February.

title: Trump insists Iran operations 'extremely' ahead of schedule
description: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that the Iran war was "extremely" ahead of schedule, as he pushed Tehran to make a deal at his first cabinet meeting since the conflict began nearly a month ago. As Tehran publicly rebuffed negotiations, Trump again cited the original timeframe of four to six weeks that he gave early on in the joint US-Israeli military offensive. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Angela Diffley tells us more.

title: US claims 'strong signs' for deal with Iran
description: Speaking at a cabinet meeting, the #Trump administration claimed there were strong signs a #deal could soon be reached with #Iran for a #ceasefire.

title: London police officer filmed in crowd intimidating Al Jazeera journalists
description: Metropolitan Police says reviewing footage of incident, reporters must be able to work without intimidation.

title: Details revealed of Board of Peace plan for Gaza disarmament
description: Plan, submitted by Board of Peace head Nikolay Mladenov, calls for gradual disarmament over eight months.
